pairs.question: What is the title of the fourth episode of the ninth season of Family Guy, and when did it originally air in the United States?
pairs.answer: The title of the fourth episode of the ninth season of Family Guy is 'Halloween on Spooner Street'. It originally aired on Fox in the United States on November 7, 2010.

pairs.question: Who are the main characters involved in the Halloween trick-or-treating subplot in 'Halloween on Spooner Street'?
pairs.answer: The main characters involved in the Halloween trick-or-treating subplot are baby Stewie and the anthropomorphic dog Brian.

pairs.question: What event leads Stewie and Brian to attempt to retrieve the stolen candy in 'Halloween on Spooner Street'?
pairs.answer: Stewie is confronted by bullies who steal his candy, which leads Stewie and Brian to attempt to take the candy back.

pairs.question: Which Family Guy characters decide to play pranks on Glenn Quagmire in 'Halloween on Spooner Street'?
pairs.answer: Peter and Joe decide to play several pranks on their neighbor Glenn Quagmire.

pairs.question: What is notable about 'Halloween on Spooner Street' in terms of its structure or subplots?
pairs.answer: 'Halloween on Spooner Street' is the first Halloween special of the Family Guy series and is one of the only episodes to feature three distinct subplots.

pairs.question: Who wrote and directed the episode 'Halloween on Spooner Street'?
pairs.answer: The episode was written by Andrew Goldberg and directed by Jerry Langford.

pairs.question: What did critics praise about 'Halloween on Spooner Street'?
pairs.answer: Critics praised the episode for its storyline and many cultural references.

pairs.question: How many homes viewed 'Halloween on Spooner Street' during its original airing according to Nielsen ratings?
pairs.answer: According to Nielsen ratings, 'Halloween on Spooner Street' was viewed in 7.97 million homes during its original airing.

pairs.question: Name at least two guest performers featured in 'Halloween on Spooner Street'.
pairs.answer: Two guest performers featured in 'Halloween on Spooner Street' are Patrick Stewart and Christina Milian.

pairs.question: Was 'Halloween on Spooner Street' submitted for an Emmy Award? If so, in which category?
pairs.answer: Yes, 'Halloween on Spooner Street' was one of five episodes submitted for consideration for an Emmy Award in the 'Outstanding Comedy Series' category in 2011.
